# Formatting figures {.unnumbered}

The two-step pattern hands you a bare plot on purpose: content first, appearance
second. This part is the second half. The constructor decided what the figure
shows; here you decide how it looks, and you do it with ordinary `ggplot2`
layers added on top.

The split pays off because the same plot serves several audiences. One object
becomes a greyscale manuscript figure, a high-contrast poster panel, or a slide,
by changing only what you add after `plot()`. The chapters cover the pieces in
turn: annotation and axis formatting, the house themes, colour scales, and
legends. Read them as a toolbox, not a sequence -- pull the one you need when a
figure is almost right but not quite.
